CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Jesus Garcia's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

The court: (1) preliminarily approves the proposed settlement of this class action with Defendant The Beach Chalet, L.P. ("Defendant"); (2) preliminarily approves the settlement as fair, adequate and reasonable; (3) preliminarily approves the gross settlement amount of $400,000.00; (4) for settlement purposes only, conditionally certifies the Class as "All current and former non-exempt employees who worked for Defendant in California ("Class") during the period from June 9, 2018, to February 2, 2025 ("Class Period"); (5) provisionally appoints Plaintiff as the representatives of the Class; (6) provisionally approves proposed service award of $10,000 (7) provisionally appoints Jean-Claude Lapuyade, JCL Law Firm, APC, Shani Zackay and Zackay Law Group, APLC, as Class Counsel; (8) provisionally approves an award of attorney's fees from the gross settlement amount to Class Counsel of $133,333.33 and expenses up to $30,000; (9) approves the form and method of notice, including notice being provided in English and Spanish; (10) directs that notice of the proposed settlement be given to the class; (11) appoints Atticus Class Action Administration as Settlement Administrator; (12) approves the proposed opt-out (so-called exclusion) procedure; and (13) sets a final approval hearing date for October 27, 2026, at 9:00 am in this department, for which Plaintiff shall file and serve a timely (under the Code of Civil Procedure) noticed motion for final approval of settlement, with courtesy copy delivered to the department within 24 hours of filing; any opposition to such motion may be in writing filed with the court at least nine court days before the hearing or provided orally at the hearing.

To the extent Plaintiff seeks different or additional relief, his motion is denied.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

The court rejects the proposed order submitted prior to the hearing. Plaintiff is ordered to resubmit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling ASAP.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File 2nd Amended Complaint / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JESUS GARCIA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File 2nd Amended Complaint

Plaintiff's "motion for leave to file second amended complaint" is granted.

"That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

The court does not pre-judge any motion defendant may file.

order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2022 Continued To May-10-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Representative Paga Claims, And Stay Action
Off Calendar Aug-31-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. JACKLAND K. HOM, Esq. of Zakay Law Group, APLC (Tel: 619-255-9047) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ALLAN J. GOMES, Esq. of Anderies & Gomes LLP (Tel: 415-217-8802) for defendant The Beach Chalet, L.P.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE BEACH CHALET, L.P.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Representative PAGA Claims, And Stay Action.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay this action is granted. Plaintiff concedes that his individual claims are arbitrable. The court declines to dismiss plaintiff's representative claims because he still has standing pursuant to Kim v. Reins Int'l California, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 73, 86. Plaintiff is allegedly an "aggrieved employee" per Labor Code sec. 2699(a), (c). He does not cease to be an aggrieved employee merely because his individual claim is compelled to arbitration. In Kim, the court held that an employee who settles and dismisses his individual claim still has standing to maintain a representative action. (See Kim, 9 Cal.5th at 80 ["Settlement of individual claims does not strip an aggrieved employee of standing, as the state's authorized representative, to pursue PAGA remedies."].) Similarly, an employee that is compelled to arbitrate his individual claims is not stripped of standing to maintain a representative action.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order which complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Cheri Bullock, CSR No. 4714, Email: cheridbullock@gmail.com, Tel: (661) 645-4048 (302/RBU)
